en.extract:
The white-winged brushfinch (Atlapetes leucopterus) is a species of bird in the family Passerellidae.


Taxonomy
The White-winged brushfinch is divided into three subspecies in two groups with the following distribution:

leucopterus/dresseri group
A. l. leucopterus – Andes of western Ecuador (south to Chimbo Valley)
A. l. dresseri – Andes of southwestern Ecuador (El Oro and Loja)
A.l. paynteri – northern Peru (northern Piura to Cajamarca)
Since 2016, Birdlife International and the IUCN have recognized paynteri as its own species, the "Cream-crowned Brushfinch".


Description
The white-winged brushfinch is a grey and white finch-like bird. Its plumage varies somewhat, with birds in the northern part of its range having a completely black face and chestnut-brown crown, while those further south have white and light crowns. Distinct white wing patches distinguish it from the Bay-crowned brushfinch.


Ecology
It is found in Ecuador and Peru. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry forest, subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest, subtropical or tropical moist montane forest, and heavily degraded former forest.


References

The '''white-winged brushfinch''' ('''''Atlapetes leucopterus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Passerellidae]].

== Taxonomy ==
The White-winged brushfinch is divided into three subspecies in two groups with the following distribution:<ref>{{Cite web |title=2024 Citation & Checklist Downloads – Clements Checklist |url=https://www.birds.cornell.edu/clementschecklist/introduction/updateindex/october-2024/2024-citation-checklist-downloads/ |access-date=2025-01-21 |website=www.birds.cornell.edu}}</ref>

* ''leucopterus/dresseri'' group
** ''A. l. leucopterus'' – [[Andes]] of western [[Ecuador]] (south to Chimbo Valley)
** ''A. l. dresseri'' – Andes of southwestern Ecuador ([[El Oro Province|El Oro]] and [[Loja, Ecuador|Loja]])
* ''A.l. paynteri'' – northern [[Peru]] (northern [[Piura]] to [[Cajamarca]])

Since 2016, [[BirdLife International|Birdlife International]] and the [[IUCN]] have recognized ''paynteri'' as its own species, the "[[Cream-crowned Brushfinch]]".<ref>{{Cite web |title=Cream-crowned Brushfinch (Atlapetes paynteri) {{!}} Summary {{!}} BirdLife International |url=https://datazone.birdlife.org/species/factsheet/cream-crowned-brushfinch-atlapetes-paynteri |access-date=2025-01-21 |website=datazone.birdlife.org}}</ref>

== Description ==
The white-winged brushfinch is a grey and white finch-like bird. Its plumage varies somewhat, with birds in the northern part of its range having a completely black face and chestnut-brown crown, while those further south have white and light crowns. Distinct white wing patches distinguish it from the [[Bay-crowned brushfinch]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=White-winged Brushfinch |url=https://ebird.org/ebird/species/wwbfin1 |access-date=2025-01-21 |website=[[eBird]] |language=en}}</ref>

== Ecology ==
It is found in [[Ecuador]] and [[Peru]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical dry forest]], [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]], subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]], and heavily [[Forest degradation|degraded]] former forest.
